title: Refactor auth system with JWT migration
description: Replace session-based auth with JWT, clean up dual auth systems (UserRole vs RbacRole), and update routes. Key changes: (1) Replace session-store.ts with jwt-store.ts using existing JwtService, (2) Consolidate UserRole (5 values) and RbacRole (3 values) into single RBAC system using admin/editor/viewer, (3) Update role-guard.ts or create rbac.ts middleware to use consistent types, (4) Ensure all routes use withAuth, (5) Document migration in docs/auth-migration.md.
existing_patterns: src/auth/jwt-service.ts - JWT signing/verification with blacklist already implemented
src/auth/withAuth.ts - HOC wrapper for route handlers using AuthService and extractBearerToken
src/auth/_auth.ts - ROLE_HIERARCHY and checkRole function for RBAC
src/middleware/role-guard.ts - requireRole decorator pattern (but uses mismatched types)
questions: Should jwt-store.ts remain in-memory (like session-store.ts) or use Payload DB for token storage (like auth-service.ts)?
The UserRole type has 5 values (admin/user/guest/student/instructor) but RbacRole has 3 (admin/editor/viewer). What is the mapping strategy - should we keep all 5 roles or consolidate to 3?
Should session-store.ts be deleted or kept deprecated for backwards compatibility during migration?
